summary: Gmail is an email service that allows users to send and receive emails, and with the Gmail API, you can integrate it into your application to automate email sending and receiving.

steps.title: Create a Gmail Account and Enable Security
steps.description: Go to google.com and log in to your account, then navigate to the Security settings page.
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the Google Account settings page with a navigation menu on the left.
steps.expected_result: You are logged into the Google Account settings page.

steps.title: Generate a Gmail Token
steps.description: Click on the Generate token button under the Security settings page.
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see a prompt to generate a token.
steps.expected_result: You have generated a Gmail token.

steps.title: Save the Gmail Token as an Environment Variable
steps.description: Save the generated token as an environment variable named GMAIL_TOKEN.
steps.screenshot_hint: You should see the token saved as an environment variable.
steps.expected_result: The GMAIL_TOKEN environment variable is set.

test_command: curl -X POST https://www.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/messages/send -H 'Authorization: Bearer $GMAIL_TOKEN' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"raw":"Your email content here"}'

common_errors.fix: Your Gmail token is invalid or has been revoked. Generate a new one from the Security settings page.
Your Gmail token does not have the required permissions. Make sure to enable the Gmail API and grant the necessary permissions.

pricing_info: Gmail API usage is free up to a certain quota, but excessive usage may require a paid Google Workspace account.
